hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework that iteratively integrates domain-specific tools (periodic table, knowledge base, and memory modules) with generative crystal structure prediction and property evaluation feedback is used for inorganic materials design, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and produce more valid, unique, and novel compositions compared to tool-free LLM variants and non-iterative diffusion-based generative models, because the structured tool-augmented reasoning enables compositionally informed, feedback-driven exploration of chemical space.
